intitule: Exercise 1: Match the Words to Their Definitions

enonce.texte: Instructions: Match each word with its correct definition.

questions.texte: Match the following terms:

sousQuestions.texte: 1. Pedestrian 2. Helmet 3. Zebra crossing 4. Seat belt 5. Traffic lights
a. A place where people cross the road safely
b. A safety device worn in a car to protect in an accident
c. Signals that control traffic at intersections
d. A protective head covering worn by cyclists or motorcyclists
e. A person who is walking, not in a vehicle

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the box.

Words: pedestrian, ambulance, first aid, addiction, food poisoning, helmet, seat belt, hygiene, speed limit, emergency exit

questions.texte: You should wear a ________ when riding a motorcycle.
The ________ in this area is 50 km/h.
A person walking on the road is called a ________.
When there is an emergency, call an ________.
________ is a condition where a person cannot stop taking a harmful substance.
Always wash your hands before eating to maintain good ________.
Eating contaminated food can cause ________.
In case of fire, use the ________.
Before driving, always fasten your ________.
He learned ________ to treat minor injuries.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Choose the correct answer for each question.

questions.texte: What should you do if you see a road accident?

sousQuestions.texte: A. Ignore it and walk away  B. Call the emergency services  C. Take photos  D. Drive away quickly
sousQuestions.reponse: B. Call the emergency services immediately.

questions.texte: What is the main cause of food poisoning?
questions.reponse: Consuming contaminated or improperly stored food.

sousQuestions.texte: A. Eating too much food  B. Consuming contaminated food  C. Drinking water  D. Exercising after eating
sousQuestions.reponse: B. Consuming contaminated or improperly stored food.

questions.texte: What is the best way to prevent smoking-related diseases?
questions.reponse: Never start smoking, or quit smoking immediately.

sousQuestions.texte: A. Smoke only occasionally  B. Use a filter  C. Never start smoking  D. Smoke in open spaces
sousQuestions.reponse: C. Never start smoking, or quit smoking immediately.

questions.texte: What does 'first aid' refer to?
questions.reponse: Emergency care given immediately to an injured person.

sousQuestions.texte: A. Long-term medical treatment  B. Immediate emergency care  C. A type of medication  D. A hospital procedure
sousQuestions.reponse: B. Immediate emergency care given to an injured person.

questions.texte: Which of the following is a symptom of a heart attack?
questions.reponse: Chest pain, shortness of breath, and sweating.

sousQuestions.texte: A. Fever and cough  B. Stomach pain  C. Chest pain and shortness of breath  D. Headache and dizziness
sousQuestions.reponse: C. Chest pain and shortness of breath, often accompanied by sweating.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Read each statement and decide if it is True or False. Correct the false statements.

questions.texte: Drug abuse can lead to serious health problems and addiction.
Wearing a seat belt is not important for short distances.
questions.reponse: False. Wearing a seat belt is important for all distances, even short ones.

questions.texte: A pedestrian is a person who drives a car.
questions.reponse: False. A pedestrian is a person walking on foot, not a driver.

questions.texte: Smoking can cause lung cancer, heart disease, and respiratory problems.
Food storage is not important for preventing food poisoning.
questions.reponse: False. Proper food storage is very important to prevent food contamination and poisoning.

questions.texte: An emergency exit is used only when there is a fire.
questions.reponse: False. An emergency exit is used in any emergency situation, not just fires.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text carefully and answer the questions that follow.

"Road accidents are a major problem in Cameroon. Every year, many people lose their lives or are seriously injured in collisions on our roads. The main causes of these accidents include speeding, drunk driving, and not wearing seat belts. Many pedestrians are also victims because they cross roads carelessly. To reduce accidents, the government has set speed limits and installed traffic lights in many cities. However, drivers and pedestrians must also take responsibility. Wearing a helmet when riding a motorcycle is mandatory in some regions. Additionally, the police regularly conduct checks to ensure drivers have valid licenses. But the best solution is for everyone to respect traffic rules."

questions.texte: What are the main causes of road accidents in Cameroon mentioned in the text?
questions.reponse: Speeding, drunk driving, and not wearing seat belts.

questions.texte: Why are pedestrians often victims of road accidents?
What measures has the government taken to reduce accidents?
questions.reponse: The government has set speed limits and installed traffic lights in many cities.

questions.texte: What is the best solution to reduce road accidents according to the text?
questions.reponse: For everyone to respect traffic rules.

questions.texte: Why is wearing a helmet mandatory in some regions?
questions.reponse: To protect motorcyclists and reduce injuries in case of accidents.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Complete the dialogue between a caller and an emergency dispatcher with appropriate words from the lesson.

Caller: Hello! There has been a serious ________ (1) on the main road.

Dispatcher: Is anyone ________ (2)?

Caller: Yes, a driver and a ________ (3) are injured. They are bleeding.

Dispatcher: Stay calm. We will send an ________ (4) immediately. Can you give us the location?

Caller: It's near the ________ (5) crossing, just before the traffic lights.

Dispatcher: Do you know how to perform ________ (6)?

Caller: No, I don't know how to give ________ (7). But the driver is unconscious.

Dispatcher: Don't move the victim. We are on our way.

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct words.
questions.reponse: 1. accident, 2. injured, 3. pedestrian, 4. ambulance, 5. zebra, 6. first aid, 7. first aid

sousQuestions.texte: What should the caller NOT do according to the dispatcher?
sousQuestions.reponse: The caller should not move the victim.
sousQuestions.explication: Moving an injured person can cause more harm, especially if they have back or neck injuries.

sousQuestions.texte: What emergency service is mentioned in the dialogue?

enonce.texte: Instructions: You are a health officer in your community. Create a short poster (8-10 sentences) to raise awareness about road safety or drug abuse prevention. Use at least 8 vocabulary words from the lesson.
images: A poster for a school campaign on road safety showing a zebra crossing, traffic lights, and a person wearing a helmet and seat belt
A health awareness poster warning about the dangers of drug abuse with illustrations of a person refusing drugs and a healthy lifestyle

questions.texte: Create your poster here.
questions.reponse: Students' answers will vary. Example (Road Safety): "BE SAFE ON THE ROAD! Always wear your seat belt and helmet. Obey traffic lights and respect the speed limit. Pedestrians should use zebra crossings. Never drive after drinking alcohol. Remember, an accident can happen at any time. Good hygiene and first aid knowledge can save lives. Let's all be responsible on the road!"

sousQuestions.texte: List the vocabulary words from the lesson that you used in your poster.
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should list at least 8 words from the lesson (e.g., seat belt, helmet, traffic lights, speed limit, pedestrian, zebra crossing, accident, first aid, alcohol).

enonce.texte: Instructions: Fill in the blanks with the correct form of the word in brackets.

questions.texte: The doctor said that smoking can cause (respire) ________ diseases.
The paramedic provided (emerge) ________ care to the accident victims.
Proper food (store) ________ helps prevent contamination.
Drug (addict) ________ is a serious community problem.
The driver was (conscious) ________ after the collision.
Good (hygiene) ________ practices can prevent the spread of diseases.
